calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-19-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Laura Woodward for (Beneficiaries) Salomon De La Bella and Rosanna De La Bella.

Mr. Ferdon stated that the only issue is stolen property, that they just worked out a settlement and that he will surcharge the administrator's fees and distribute among the heirs. The Court asked why a $40,000.00 reserve is necessary. Mr. Ferdon stated that their tax accountant is requesting that amount be held, that they have no information to go on, that the IRS will not grant an early audit and that they will need to hold the amount for 3 years. Ms. Woodward stated that the heirs are in poor health and would like the order signed as soon as possible. The accounting petition was taken under submission. Mr. Ferdon is to submit a new proposed order.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Lopes. (Not Reported)

Petition To Determine Heirship, And To Recognize Judgment Of The District Court Of Amsterdam, The Netherlands, As Res Judicata And Collatereal Estoppel
Off Calendar Dec-16-2014 Off Calendar

Petition To Determine Heirship, And To Recognize Judgment Of The District Court Of Amsterdam, The Netherlands, As Res Judicata And Collatereal Estoppel
Off Calendar Oct-28-2014 Continued To Dec-16-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Cain for Petitioner. Laura Woodward for Salomon de la Bella, Rosanna de la Bella van Doorn and Kemp & Associates.

The Court stated that it does not think the Netherland judgment is res judicata or provides collateral estoppel that Tys was the Father of Jules. Mr. Cain requested a two full day or more evidentiary hearing. Ms. Woodward argued that an evidentiary hearing is not needed and that the petition should be denied. Mr. Cain is to file a brief as to whether there is a factual issue which would entitle a Party to an evidentiary hearing by November 25, 2014. Ms. Woodward is to file a response by December 9, 2014. The Petition to Determine Heirship, etc. was continued to December 16, 2014 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 to see whether the matter should be set for an evidentiary.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Petition To Determine Heirship, And To Recognize Judgment Of The District Court Of Amsterdam, The Netherlands, As Res Judicata And Collatereal Estoppel
Off Calendar Oct-14-2014 Continued To Oct-28-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances - Thomas Cain for Christiane Tijs, Jonathon Ferdon for the Public Administrator, Laura Woodward for Salomon de la Bella and Rosanna de la Bella van Doorn, objectors.

Mr. Cain requested a continuance as objections were just filed Thursday October 9, and Monday October 13 was a court holiday. He stated his reply had been dropped off downstairs (due to the clerk strike, all filings had to be dropped off, not filed). The Court indicated a continuance was appropriate in order for it to review all of the pleadings. Ms. Woodward stated a continuance was not necessary and the petition should be heard. The Court stated the petition would not be heard on its merits as the Court had not had time to review all of the pleadings, due to the late filing of the objections. The petition was continued to October 28, 2014.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Sandy Hilton.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1969-17th Ave., San Francisco, Ca 94116)
Off Calendar Jun-26-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for Public Administrator.

Mr. Ferdon presented an endorsed copy of a waiver of notice from the Norway Claimants. The sale was confirmed at $977,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed May 24, 2013. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to receipt and review of the order). Bond is not required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Mann.
